game_name: Bubsy in: Claws Encounters of the Furred Kind

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate 2D side-scrolling levels, jump on enemies (Woolies), collect yarn balls for extra lives, progress through 16 levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Maintaining lives through enemy avoidance and hazard navigation while collecting optional yarn ball pickups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Fast-paced platforming adventure as Bubsy the cat defending Earth's yarn supply from alien invaders
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentional design goal to be 'as fast as Sonic and as deep as Mario' - bridging speed-focused and precision-focused platformer philosophies. Heavy marketing positioning it as next-gen platformer competitor. Strong personality via voice acting and catchphrases rather than silent protagonist.

difficulty.lives_system: Starts with 9 lives; collecting 500 yarn balls grants extra life
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Yarn ball collection provides optional difficulty mitigation through life stockpiling

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression; secret paths exist (added based on child playtester feedback)
replayability_hooks: Secret underground tube ways in levels
Yarn ball collection for high scores and extra lives
Multiple versions (SNES, Genesis, Windows) with distinct characteristics

world_building_techniques: Protagonist voice acting with distinctive personality (Brian Silva, Looney Tunes-inspired)
Catchphrase delivery ('What could possibly go wrong?')
Character-driven premise (Bubsy's personal yarn ball collection stakes)
Alien invader antagonists (Woolies) as clear thematic opposition

danger_signals: Banana peel hazards (censored replacement for catnip mechanic)
environmental_storytelling: Yarn ball scattered throughout levels as collectibles
Underground tube network passages suggesting hidden infrastructure
